title: One Tap Away (Riverboat Bad Boys Remix)

identity.vocalStyle: raspy street delivery with call-and-response ad-libs
identity.styleSentence: A gritty hardcore gangsta rap track running at a mid-tempo 95 BPM that fuses grimy 90s East Coast boom bap drums with dusty upright bass stabs and sinister piano loops to create a black-and-white 1928 riverboat noir atmosphere, featuring raspy aggressive male vocals over an instrumentation palette of muted trumpets, creepy xylophone hits, vinyl hiss, and foghorn-like low-end frequencies.
identity.summary: A gritty noir critique on digital isolation where social media connections replace genuine intimacy, set against a cinematic backdrop of dirty dockside crime and rubber-hose animation chaos.

keywords.imageryPrompt: A solitary figure in a grainy black and white noir room, surrounded by floating digital contact icons that refuse to connect like broken chain links.
A gloved hand slamming against an old wooden dock post, sending ripples through dirty water where a reflection of the hand does not match the movement above.
Piles of gold coins turning to dust and ash instantly under harsh dockside floodlights, emphasizing temporary fame over substance.
A finger rapidly swiping past a crying face on an old CRT screen until the image blurs into static noise and darkness.
Tiny, cold pinpricks of light in a vast foggy sky far above a gritty riverboat deck where humans are just small specks below.
Dozens of glowing user handles falling like dead leaves onto a muddy dock floor, leaving no footprints or warmth behind them.
